author: Kr1gs
content:
@[AEP] Dez 
Could elaborate on what you mean with true NFT's?

The issue with the necklace is that it was minted by FTX US.
The current marketplace is already NFT and FT ready.

Any item currently from Star Atlas is what most people call a SFT which is actually an Ethereum term. 
Best explanation so far is this article i.m.o. :
https:// lorisleiva.com/owning-digital-assets-in-solana/how-nfts-are-represented-in-solana
Other resources:
https:// docs.metaplex.com/programs/token-metadata/accounts
https:// docs.metaplex.com/programs/token-metadata/token-standard

Issues with NFT's that contain external data will always be a problem, especially on proprietary storage solutions. 
If nobody maintains the storage (pay for the hosting) things will disappear like what happend with the FTX mints.

This is what people need to understand with 'True NFT's' most of the NFT's are not the "art" but only the part that is truly on the blockchain, which is not much most of the time.
Just a receipt that you bought something and you are the true owner of that receipt.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: There seems like a number of possible additional revenue streams for the UE5 experience.  However, one major barrier to entry for the UE5 experience is, so far, it requires a beefy computer especially on the video card side.  If someone already has a usable gaming PC for the Showroom, they can probably also afford to purchase their own ships.  I realize this doesn't apply to the larger ships but if you have already purchased a multiple thousand of dollar PC rig, getting some ships doesn't seem like much of a stretch to that person's budget.  Plus, if you have a large ship, seems like you would be probably engaging it in your own gameplay then just renting it out.

author: Oscar GF | Starke FinanceUstur
content: Actually, I disagree.  Yes there are a lot of PC gamers but they don't represent all the possible gamers or people that could be interested in Star Atlas.  Given that the UE5 version of Star Atlas isn't the first part of the Star Atlas ecosystem planned to be available, I think the price of a PC will be a limited factor to bringing people into the Star Atlas metaverse.    If the goal was just to create a PC game with blockchain mechanics, that is fine.  But to be a much more accessible gaming experience, I think price is a factor and currently, to me, the UE5 experience has too high of a cost.  Ideally, the future UE5 should be able to be run on a decent laptop with integrated graphics.  I realize that this isn't actually something that the team may have control of since using UE5.  But, my point is other costs to entry can be limiting to adoption of Star Atlas.

author: ZiggyMUD
content:
Dear Start Atlas Community I've been thinking a lot about the POLIS DAO and what it will mean to the Star Atlas metaverse. I believe if the POLIS DAO succeeds Star Atlas as a Web3 game is more likely to succeed. This is why I feel that it would be beneficial to create an additional revenue source for the POLIS DAO.
I am proposing that the Star Atlas Merch Store should be funded and managed by the POLIS DAO. This could give the POLIS DAO more funding to help the Star Atlas Metaverse grow! The merch earnings bought by the community could go directly back into the amazing community events or development managed by the POLIS DAO.

I am willing to help fund this Star Atlas Merch Store development  until the POLIS DAO is able to use it's treasury money, but will need help if anyone thinks this is a good idea and wants to contribute their skills or knowledge.

Would love to hear your thoughts?

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content:
@SW4GN3R @bunthius 

An AEP member shared his experience with a related product. Thought you'd like to see his thoughts. Cheers. 

[AEP] Jadjad — Today at 2:50 PM
I have a thought that may be completely unrelated.

I've been using another dApp for another game.  DFKquester is the dApp for DeFi Kingdoms.

One neat thing DFKQ does is that is just formulates the transactions for any activity it does with my wallet.  I use MetaMask as the wallet.  MetaMask shows you the contract address you are interacting with on every transaction.  MetaMask also allows you to put a nickname to the transaction.

Ok, so you go to the game's official website.  You send a hero on a quest.  When MetaMask presents the transaction, you save the contract address to your contacts in MetaMask with the nickname "DFK-QuestContract".

Now, you go over to the third party dApp and send a hero on a quest using their interface.  When MetaMask presents the transaction for you, the wallet app automatically resolves the contract address to the name "DFK-QuestContract".  This way I can be confident the 3rd party is giving me a transaction that interacts with the Official Quest Contract.  Also, I am never giving the third party my private key.  MetaMask is handling the key signing.

It is a very elegant way to add efficiency to routine contract interactions (resupplying in this case).  Granted it does not have full "botlike" capabilities for total automation.  It does however find a "middle ground" for efficiency without sacrificing the handling of your private keys.

reply.preview: @SW4GN3R @bunthius An AEP member shared his experience with a related product. Thought you'd like to see his thoughts. Cheers. [AEP] Jadjad — Today at 2:50 PM I have a thought that may be completely unrelated. I've been using another dApp for another game. DFKquester is the dApp for DeFi Kingdoms. One neat thing DFKQ does is that is just formulates the transactions for any activity it does with my wallet. I use MetaMask as the wallet. MetaMask shows you the contract address you are interacting with on every transaction. MetaMask also allows you to put a nickname to the transaction. Ok, so you go to the game's official website. You send a hero on a quest. When MetaMask presents the transaction, you save the contract address to your contacts in MetaMask with the nickname "DFK-QuestContract". Now, you go over to the third party dApp and send a hero on a quest using their interface. When MetaMask presents the transaction for you, the wallet app automatically resolves the contract address to the name "DFK-QuestContract". This way I can be confident the 3rd party is giving me a transaction that interacts with the Official Quest Contract. Also, I am never giving the third party my private key. MetaMask is handling the key signing. It is a very elegant way to add efficiency to routine contract interactions (resupplying in this case). Granted it does not have full "botlike" capabilities for total automation. It does however find a "middle ground" for efficiency without sacrificing the handling of your private keys.

author: Oscar GF | Starke FinanceUstur
content: Maybe I was moving  the conversation more to my world but as a company that you produce product X, you need an IT stack to deliver it. What I meant is that you own the decision of how to deploy it based on your circumstances, if you prefer onpremise vs IaaS, Nutanix vs VMWare etc, so more than compete, you consume the IT services around. In my company (no IT business oriented) we recently made an assessment where moving everything to the cloud (the IaaS triumvirate GCP, AWS & Azure) would cost many times the cost compared to onpremise, why? because specific needs and because having 200PBs & 30000VMs generating tones of traffic in a Cloud provider would literally cost billions, so as a client it is always look for the right tool for the right work. As IT provider that's a whole different story, as you plan to compete with other companies in that IT stack to potentially offer that service to others. Web3 itself is still positioning as it is not very clear which layers will cover because you can do many stuff, but anyway there will always other layers that you will need in parallel to build the whole solution stack (IPs and service providers, VMs, storage, DataCenters etc). Anyway, amazing years upfront mounted on my ballad!

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: There was a case a few months back of some wallet keeping seed phrases in one of their logging tools.  I don't have enough technical knowledge about non-hardware wallets but they seem like there is always a possibility of vulnerabilities.  Hopefully Ledger is immune to these issues.  But, the bigger problem is crypto wallets are a big barrier to entry.   They are great if the goal was just storing cryptocurrency or NFTs that you seldom need to access.  But, with other uses like Star Atlas, they are need to be accessed much more frequently.  This opens up the possibility of people getting lazy and just approving things or scam attacks like fake NFTs and airdrops.  Hopefully time will bring solutions to this first-mover concerns.

author: Bonnie [Boof]
content:
My faith is being tested y'all. If you know me you know I've been a supporter since almost the beginning of Star Atlas. Held on through the bear market and FTX debacle without complaint. Lately I just can't stop questioning whether or not SA will be around in 2-3 years. We can't count on another bull market to save us. As an outside investor, I would be extremely hesitant to partner with ATMTA due to the lack of regulatory clarity around NFTs, and the associated risks. Very large sums and long timescales will be needed to create the full UE5 MMO experience. I'm excited for SAGE but also aware that it will be in development without full features for years. I worry we'll run out of runway before the product is far enough along to attract a significant playerbase. 

Can someone more bullish give me their take? I don't want to give up but I need more reasons to justify holding on. Didn't post in genchat because I sincerely don't want to FUD.

author: MrTixlUstur
content: Yeah, there really is no answer that anyone can give you to make you feel better. Its a high risk, high reward situation. I think if the team got their back against the wall, they would just need to lower their current evaluation of Atmta to raise capital to keep this thing going...but otherwise no one can give you an answer on the regulatory front. At the end of the day, only risk what youre willing to accept as a total loss and anything better than that would be a surprise benefit. I poured millions into this cause its a great concept and someone (if its not Star Atlas) will develop a Sustainable Metaverse Economy for the masses..as of right now, Star Altas is one of the early leaders in that space...and the winners will reap huge gains for their supporters...
